name: Dave Edmunds
description:
'''David William Edmunds''' (born 15 April 1944) is a Welsh retired singer, songwriter, guitarist and [[record producer]]. He is mainly associated with [[Pub rock (United Kingdom)|pub rock]] and [[New wave music|new wave]], having produced many [[hit record|hits]] in these genres during the 1970s and early 1980s.

Edmunds first played in a band with his older brother Geoff when he was 10. This led to a succession of bands with others. In 1968, he was a member of [[Love Sculpture]] who had a top 5 hit with [[Sabre Dance]]. Edmunds embarked on a solo career after Love Sculpture. His 1970 [[List of Christmas number one singles (UK)|Christmas single]], "[[I Hear You Knocking]]", was certified [[Music recording sales certification|gold]] and sold over three million copies. 

In 1976, Edmunds formed the group [[Rockpile]], with [[Nick Lowe]], [[Billy Bremner (musician)|Billy Bremner]] and [[Terry Williams (drummer)|Terry Williams]]. Due to contractual arrangements, the group did not record as Rockpile until 1980. Between 1976 and 1980, Edmunds enjoyed success with Nick Lowe's "[[I Knew the Bride]]", [[Elvis Costello]]'s "[[Girls Talk (Elvis Costello song)|Girls Talk]]", Hank DeVito's "[[Queen of Hearts (Hank DeVito song)|Queen of Hearts]]", [[Graham Parker]]'s "[[Crawling from the Wreckage]]", and [[Melvin Endsley]]'s "[[Singing the Blues]]". After Rockpile released their first LP under their own name, ''[[Seconds of Pleasure]]'' (1980), the band split. Edmunds spent the 1980s collaborating with and producing an assortment of artists, including [[Paul McCartney]], [[King Kurt]], [[Stray Cats]], [[Fabulous Thunderbirds]], and [[Status Quo (band)|Status Quo]]. His 1983 release, ''[[Information (Dave Edmunds album)|Information]]'' produced a US [[Top 40]] hit with "[[Slipping Away (Dave Edmunds song)|Slipping Away]]". It was followed in 1984 by the album ''[[Riff Raff (album)|Riff Raff]]''. 

Edmunds recorded less frequently after the mid-1980s. In 2013, he released a compilation album "''...Again''" followed, in 2015, with an instrumental album ''On Guitar... Dave Edmunds: Rags & Classics''. After playing a final show in July 2017, he was reported to have retired from the music business.
